methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $110M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Manna Aero is a Series B drone and autonomous systems company that has raised $110M to date. The valuation universe for drones and autonomous vehicles consists of 7 private peers, including Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Asylon Robotics, with no comparable public companies available for direct benchmarking.

A stage-progression methodology was applied, using the $110M in total capital raised as a valuation proxy. This method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage progressions, yielding an implied valuation of $209M. A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient comparable data; while four same-sector peers exist, only one occupies the same stage, and adjacent-stage and similar-tier filtering did not yield the minimum three datapoints required for robust median-based triangulation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $178M to $240M, centered on $209M. This single-method triangulation carries material uncertainty around the appropriate step-up multiple for Manna Aero's stage and market conditions, as well as around the use of total raised as a valuation proxy. The narrow peer set in drones and autonomous systems limits cross-validation. Key variables affecting valuation include trajectory toward revenue and unit economics relative to the broader cohort.
